summary: Completed testing of Contact form and Admin Messages tab features. Contact form submission works correctly with success feedback. Admin Messages tab displays messages with 'new' badge count, expandable details, and status change functionality. Found one backend bug: status filter not working correctly.

critical.issue: Status filter not working - when filtering by status (e.g., status=New), the query is overwritten by $ne: Archived logic on line 81, causing all non-archived messages to be returned regardless of status filter
critical.fix_suggestion: Change logic to combine status filter with archived exclusion using $and operator

action_items: Fix status filter bug in /app/backend/routes/contact.py - the status query parameter is being overwritten by the include_archived logic
critical_code_review_comments: Bug in contact.py lines 78-81: When status filter is provided, it gets overwritten by the $ne: Archived query, making status filtering ineffective
Contact form properly validates required fields (name, email, message) on both frontend and backend
Admin Messages tab correctly shows 'new' badge count and updates when status changes
Email notifications via Resend are implemented but require RESEND_API_KEY (skipped silently without key)

success_rate.backend: 93% (14/15 tests passed - 1 failed due to status filter bug)

seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_ prefixed contact messages and newsletter subscriptions for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Contact form and Admin Messages tab tested. Status filter has a bug that needs fixing. All other features (form submission, message expansion, status change, newsletter signup) working correctly.

details: Form displays all fields (name, email, phone, service, message)
Required field validation works
Form submits to backend successfully
Success feedback shown ('Message Sent' button state + toast)
Form fields cleared after successful submission
Messages tab visible in admin dashboard
'X new' badge shows count of new messages
Messages list shows name, email, service type, date
Click to expand shows full details (email link, phone, project type, message)
Status change buttons work (New -> Read -> Replied -> Archived)
Reply button opens mailto link
Delete button available
Filter dropdown present with status options
BUG: Filtering by status does not work - all messages shown regardless of filter selection
Root cause: Backend code overwrites status query with $ne: Archived
'Join the Pack' section on Contact page
Email input and Subscribe button work
Success message 'You're in. Welcome to the pack.' displays after subscription
Toast notification also shown
POST /api/contact - creates message (PASSED)
GET /api/contact - lists messages (PASSED)
GET /api/contact/{id} - gets specific message (PASSED)
PUT /api/contact/{id} - updates status (PASSED)
DELETE /api/contact/{id} - archives message (PASSED)
DELETE /api/contact/{id}?permanent=true - permanently deletes (PASSED)
GET /api/contact?status=X - filter by status (FAILED - bug)
POST /api/newsletter - subscribes email (PASSED)

mocked_apis.resend_email: Email notifications via Resend are implemented but require RESEND_API_KEY in .env. Without the key, emails are skipped silently. This is expected behavior.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Status filter not working in GET /api/contact
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In /app/backend/routes/contact.py lines 78-81, when a status filter is provided, it sets query['status'] = status. However, immediately after, if include_archived is False (default), it overwrites query['status'] with {'$ne': 'Archived'}, completely ignoring the original status filter.
reproduction_steps: 1. Create messages with different statuses (New, Read)
3. Observe that messages with status 'Read' are also returned
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Combine both conditions using $and operator: if status and not include_archived: query['$and'] = [{'status': status}, {'status': {'$ne': 'Archived'}}]
